title: Phlegmariurus tetragonus
taxonomy: Phlegmariurus tetragonus is classified under the family Lycopodiaceae. It belongs to the order Lycopodiales and the class Lycopodiopsida. This species is further categorized as a member of the genus Phlegmariurus. The classification of Phlegmariurus tetragonus is also reflected in its binomial nomenclature, indicating its specific and generic names. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics, as well as its evolutionary relationships with other plants.

side_effects: Phlegmariurus tetragonus is a type of plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. intro: Phlegmariurus tetragonus, also known as the four-angled climbing fern, is a type of plant that belongs to the lycopodiophyta class, characterized by its fern-like appearance and ability to climb using aerial roots. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce oxidative stress, and improve cardiovascular health.
